model_narrative: We plan a tour over a set of cities, choosing for each ordered pair of cities whether the direct arc from one to the other is used. Every used arc carries a known travel cost. The objective is to choose the arcs so that the total travel cost of all chosen arcs is as small as possible.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the cities to be visited; an arc runs from an origin city to a destination city, both drawn from this same set

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city, in cost units per arc

vars.name: x
vars.doc: takes the value 1 when the direct arc from the first city to the second city is used, and 0 otherwise

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Each city is left exactly once. For every city, considering all the arcs that start at that city and go to any city, exactly one of those arcs is used.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Each city is reached exactly once. For every city, considering all the arcs that come into that city from any city, exactly one of those arcs is used.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: No city connects directly to itself. For every city, the arc that starts and ends at that same city is never used.

query: We're looking at a routing rule that ties the city-4/city-5 area to the city-6/city-7 depot pair: at least one direct hop has to run between those two areas in some direction. Add that requirement and tell me what the assignment total becomes.
expected_pyomo: model.link_4567 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] + model.x[j, i] for i in ('i4', 'i5') for j in ('i6', 'i7')) >= 1)

query: Suppose cities 6 and 7 can no longer be assigned directly to each other in either direction. Block that pairing and let me see the new assignment cost.

query: There's a proposal on the table that cities 8 and 9 can't just service each other and call it done — together they have to send at least one outgoing link to some city outside that pair. Put that in and tell me what the assignment ends up costing.
expected_pyomo: model.leave_89 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] for i in ['i8', 'i9'] for j in model.I if j not in ('i8', 'i9')) >= 1)

query: We're considering a limit on how clustered the eastern cities 4, 5, 6 and 7 are allowed to be: no more than two of the used arcs can stay entirely within that group of four. Apply that cap and let me see the resulting assignment total.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_east = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] for i in ('i4', 'i5', 'i6', 'i7') for j in ('i4', 'i5', 'i6', 'i7')) <= 2)

query: Cities 4 and 5 just ping-pong straight off each other in this solution. Wouldn't it be better to stop them from forming that little closed pair and route through them as part of a longer chain? What's pulling them into a two-city loop?

query: City 1 and city 12 are stuck in their own back-and-forth pair here. Couldn't the plan break that closed loop and weave them into the rest of the route instead? What's the trade-off keeping them locked together?
